learning_outcomes.description: Explain electric and magnetic circuits related to electrical machines.
Apply various tests for parameter calculations of models of electrical machines.
Develop equivalent circuits of electric machines for given conditions and analyse them.
Distinguish different modes of bulk electrical energy transmission and their inherent limitations.
Comprehend the distribution and utilization side of electrical energy and appropriate load management strategies.
Develop an overall understanding of how electrical energy conversion, transmission and utilisation work in power systems.

assessments.task_description:
The practical assessment component of the course will evaluate the students understanding of the operation and measurement of energy conversion devices including transformers, synchronous machines, induction machines and DC machines. There are five distinct labs associated with this assessment item as follows:

- Laboratory 1 (6%): Three-phase load measurement.

-Laboratory 2 (6%): Transformer Testing.

- Laboratory 3 (6%): Induction Machines.

- Laboratory 4 (6%): Synchronous Machines.

- Laboratory 5 (6%): DC Machines. Due date:

A complete lab schedule and deadline details are available in the course Blackboard site as well.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: There is no submission required outside the lab session. During the lab students will have to conduct experiments, record reading and answer questions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This will be an onsite exam and will cover the first five topics, including Induction Motors. This will be a closed book, timed exam conducted face-to-face. The exam will be held during Monday's regular lecture period in Week 8.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures on Energy conversion and utilization

Details of lectures are available in course blackboard page. All the lectures will be on campus. The following topics will be covered: (1) Introduction to Practical Power and Energy Conversion System; (2) Three phase power system; (3) Magnetics circuits; (4) Electrical Power Transformers; (5) Fundamentals of rotating machines; (6) Induction machines; (7) Synchronous machines; (8) DC machines; (9) Transmission of electricity; (10) Distribution Systems and Electrical Energy Utilisation.
